battleBot.title: Battle Bot
battleBot.description: Engine continuously trained on 2M+ battle outcomes · ELO rankings · Win-rate matrices · Archetype matchups
battleBot.readyDesc: Build or load a team, configure your opponent pool, then run the Monte Carlo battle simulation. The engine will play your team against every opponent with intelligent AI decisions, and give you a full breakdown of your team's strengths and weaknesses.
battleBot.noSpeedControl: No speed control - consider Tailwind or Trick Room
battleBot.subtitle: Champions Lab Powered · Gold Tier
battleBot.longDescription: The most advanced VGC battle simulator available - fed with 2,000,000+ simulated battles, full damage calculation engine, intelligent AI decision-making, abilities, items, weather, terrain, Trick Room, Tailwind, status conditions, and real-time battle replay. Test your team against {metaTeams}+ meta teams and hundreds of randomized opponents.
battleBot.topThreats: Top Threats - Pokémon that beat you most
battleBot.localChanges: Changes are local to this session only

insights.strongTeam: Strong team! Focus on preserving your win condition in best-of-3
insights.topLeads: Your top leads give the best matchup spread - stick to them

meta.title: Meta Analysis
meta.description: Usage stats, teams, and core pairs from ranked ladder data and {count} community tournaments ({teams} teams).
meta.noTrackedCorePairs: No tracked core pairs in this team
meta.liveUsageDesc: Live usage data from Pokémon Champions ranked doubles ladder and community tournaments. These are the Pokémon real players are picking right now.
meta.tournamentWinningDesc: Top-placing teams from Pokémon Champions community tournaments. Click any team for full breakdown with sets.
meta.enginePredictedDesc: Teams predicted from 2M simulated battles + tournament data
meta.officialUsageDesc: Real usage data from Nintendo's Champions Ranked Doubles ladder. Rankings reflect actual player pick rates across all skill brackets.
meta.communityUsageDesc: Real team data from {count} official & community Pokémon Champions tournaments on Limitless. Shows actual competitive pick rates and top-8 conversion.
meta.mlSimulationDesc: ELO from {count} battles. Auto-updated after each engine run.
meta.antiMetaDesc: Pokémon that counter the current meta. Scored by win rate vs top 15 threats. Auto-updated after engine runs.
meta.tournamentTeamsDesc: Top teams from {count}+ official and community Pokémon Champions tournaments. Click any team for full breakdown.
meta.curatedTeamsDesc: Hand-built competitive teams with full sets, items, and SP spreads. Ready to load into the Team Builder.
meta.antiMetaTeamsDesc: Counter-teams designed to beat the most common tournament strategies. Built around top anti-meta cores.
meta.corePairDesc: The strongest 2-Pokémon cores in competitive play. Each core pair is rated by tournament win rate, usage, and how well they complement each other. ML simulation cores are shown separately below.
meta.archetypeMatchupDesc: How each archetype performs against the others. Win rates above 55% are highlighted green, below 45% red. This data combines tournament results with ML simulation insights.
meta.moveWinRateDesc: Move win rates from 2,000,000 ML simulated battles. Higher win rate means teams using this move won more often. Usage count shows total appearances across all battles.
meta.speedTiersDesc: Complete speed tier chart for all {count} Pokémon. Calculated at Level 50 with max IVs (31) and max SP investment (32) where applicable. Mega Evolutions cannot hold items — Choice Scarf columns show N/A.
meta.noFallingDesc: The meta is stable — all used Pokémon are above 50% win rate

antiMeta.strategy: Built around {name} ({score} anti-meta score). Counters {c1} and {c2}.

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: The people behind Champions Lab
about.alphaDescription: This project is in active development. Things may break or change. It's 100% free, open source (MIT), and community-driven.
about.discordDescription: Report bugs, suggest features, discuss the meta, and connect with other trainers
about.supportBmc: Support Us on Buy Me a Coffee
about.followXDesc: Stay up to date with the latest Champions Lab updates, VGC news, and community highlights.
about.contactDesc: Found a bug? Have a suggestion? Just want to say hi? We'd love to hear from you!

mission.p1: Champions Lab was born from a simple idea: competitive Pokémon tools should be free and accessible to everyone. We're a small team of passionate Pokémon fans who have been playing VGC for years, and we wanted to create the ultimate companion for Pokémon Champions 2026.
mission.p2: Every feature you see - the Pokédex, the Team Builder, the 2,000,000+ battle simulation engine, the Meta analysis, PokéSchool - was built with love during our free time. We don't charge anything. No ads, no paywalls, no premium tiers. This project is and will always be 100% free.
mission.p3: We believe the competitive Pokémon community deserves high-quality tools without having to pay for them. If you find Champions Lab useful, the best way to support us is to share it with your friends and fellow trainers!

credits.p1: Champions Lab Team - Design, development, battle engine, data curation, and everything in between. We're a small but dedicated team that pours hours into making this the best competitive Pokémon tool out there.
credits.p2: Huge thanks to the competitive Pokémon community for the constant inspiration, the tournament organizers who make VGC possible, and every player who has shared a team, written a guide, or helped a newcomer.
credits.p3: Special thanks to everyone who has reported bugs, suggested features, or just said something nice. This project exists because of you.

contribute.p1: Champions Lab is open source and we welcome contributions! Whether you're a developer, a designer, a competitive player with data to share, or just someone who spotted a bug - we'd love your help.
contribute.p2: You can report bugs, suggest features, or contribute code. Use the contact form below to get in touch, or open an issue on our <github>GitHub repository</github>.
contribute.p3: If you'd like to support us financially, every little bit helps keep the project alive:

changelog.description: Thanks for the support  -  we're updating the website as fast as possible to adapt to everything new from Pokémon Champions!

season.ruleDoublesDesc: All battles are played in Double Battle format - 2v2 on the field at all times. The standard competitive Pokémon format used in official VGC tournaments worldwide.
season.ruleBring6Desc: Build a team of 6 Pokémon, then select 4 to bring into each battle after seeing your opponent's team during Team Preview. Strategy starts before the first move.
season.ruleLevel50Desc: All Pokémon are automatically set to Level 50 for battles, ensuring a fair playing field regardless of your Pokémon's actual level.
season.ruleStatPointsDesc: Champions replaces IVs and EVs with a streamlined Stat Point system. You get 64 total points to distribute (max 32 per stat). Each point adds ~1 stat at Level 50. No RNG, pure strategy.
season.ruleMegaDesc: Mega Evolution returns! One Pokémon per team can Mega Evolve during battle, gaining boosted stats and sometimes a new typing or ability. A game-changing mechanic from Gen 6 & 7.
season.ruleNoDupePokemonDesc: Each Pokémon species can only appear once on your team. Known as the Species Clause - a core rule in all official competitive formats.
season.ruleNoDupeItemsDesc: Each held item can only be used once per team. Known as the Item Clause - forces diverse item choices and prevents stacking powerful items like Leftovers or Choice Scarf.
season.ruleTimerDesc: Each game has a 20-minute time limit. If time runs out, the player with more remaining Pokémon wins. Prevents stalling and keeps games fast-paced.

paste.noTeam: No team data found. Use the Share button in the Team Builder to generate a paste link.
